name: Check PackageReference Versions
description: Guards the Central Package Management rule: warns if a Version attribute is added directly to a <PackageReference> element in any .csproj file instead of using Directory.Packages.props.

then.prompt:
A .csproj file was just saved. Read the file and scan every <PackageReference> element.

If any <PackageReference> has a Version="..." attribute (e.g. <PackageReference Include="Foo" Version="1.0.0" />), warn the user:

> ❌ CPM violation: <PackageReference Include="{name}" Version="{version}" /> has a hardcoded version. Remove the Version attribute from the .csproj and ensure a <PackageVersion Include="{name}" Version="{version}" /> entry exists in Directory.Packages.props instead.

If no violations are found, do nothing.
